query: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) financing, capital raise, cash position and going concern status in 2026. Has the company raised significant capital or secured funding in Q2 2026 (April-June 2026)? What is their current cash runway?
answer:
Research complete. Here is a summary of the key findings on Virgin Galactic (SPCE) financing and cash position in 2026:

**Did SPCE raise capital in Q2 2026 (April-June)?** Yes — approximately **$52 million in gross ATM (at-the-market) equity proceeds in April 2026**, with ~$87M remaining on the program. This was dilutive drip-financing via its Jefferies ATM facility, not a single large secured funding round.

**Cash position:** **$251 million** in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of March 31, 2026 (down from ~$373M in current assets at Dec 31, 2025).

**Cash runway:** No explicit management guidance, but the math implies roughly **3-4 quarters** at current burn. Q1 2026 free cash flow was -$93.3M, and Q2 2026 FCF is guided to -$87M to -$92M with sequential improvement expected. Starting Q2 with ~$251M + ~$52M April ATM (~$300M) at ~$85-93M/quarter burn extends runway into roughly mid-2027, aligning with targeted first commercial spaceflight (Q4 2026) and revenue ramp (2027). The company depends on continued ATM issuance and dilution.

**Going concern:** **No going-concern doubt disclosure** appears in the Q1 2026 earnings release or referenced filings. CEO framed cash as "maintained at appropriate levels as we work through the final quarters of our pre-revenue phase."

**Context:** Heavy dilution (diluted shares up from ~35.4M in Q1 2025 to ~79.5M in Q1 2026); prior December 2025 convertible note refinancing; stock at ~$2.55 on July 7, 2026 (~71% below 52-week high), after a volatile June that included a spike to $7.52 on a Q1 beat and reports of a new strategic investor.

One caveat: the most recent audited/hard financial data is from Q1 2026 (reported May 14, 2026). Q2 2026 results (covering April-June) would not yet be reported as of the July 7, 2026 access date, so the ~$52M April ATM raise is the confirmed Q2 capital event, with any May/June ATM activity not yet disclosed.

key_facts: Virgin Galactic held $251 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026), down from ~$373 million in current assets at Dec 31, 2025.
In Q2 2026 (April 2026), Virgin Galactic raised approximately $52 million in gross ATM equity proceeds, with ~$87 million remaining on the ATM program afterward.
Q1 2026 free cash flow was -$93.3 million; Q2 2026 FCF guidance is -$87M to -$92M, with sequential improvement expected thereafter.
No going-concern doubt disclosure appears in Virgin Galactic's Q1 2026 earnings release or referenced 2026 SEC filings.
The company's cash runway is roughly 3-4 quarters at current burn (~$85-93M/quarter) absent further raises, extending into roughly mid-2027; it relies on ongoing dilutive ATM issuance.
A December 2025 convertible senior note refinancing (announced Dec 9, closed Dec 18, 2025) included a registered direct offering; a Jan 9, 2026 424B3 restored $45.6M of ATM capacity.
Diluted share count grew from ~35.4M (Q1 2025) to ~79.5M (Q1 2026), reflecting heavy dilution from equity issuance.
SPCE traded at ~$2.55 on July 7, 2026 (market cap ~$328M), ~71% below its 52-week high of $8.90; it spiked to $7.52 on June 1, 2026 on a Q1 beat and reports of a new strategic investor.
Q1 2026 GAAP net loss was -$64.7 million (EPS -$0.81) on revenue of only $0.2 million.
Company offered to redeem ~$10 million of near-term 2026 notes via stock; ~$202M remains on first-lien notes due December 2028.
Operational milestones: Delta-class flight testing targeted Q3 2026, first rocket-powered spaceflight Q4 2026, commercial ramp in 2027.
